test.name: Ancestry search request should route to search-external-sites
test.description: User asks to search Ancestry for Patrick Flynn. Ancestry is a commercial site explicitly listed in the skill description's Do NOT use clause. The skill must route away without calling record_search — search-external-sites handles commercial databases.

input.user_message: Can you search Ancestry for Patrick Flynn in the 1850 census?

negative.explanation: Ancestry is a commercial database explicitly named in the skill's Do NOT use clause (alongside MyHeritage, FindMyPast, FindAGrave, Newspapers.com). search-records only searches FamilySearch. Calling record_search here would be wrong — that tool queries FamilySearch, not Ancestry.

judge_context: This is a NEGATIVE routing test. The ONLY correct behavior is to call Skill('search-external-sites') and stop — nothing else.
Must NOT call record_search — that tool queries FamilySearch, not Ancestry
Must NOT construct a URL, must NOT write any log entry to research.json, must NOT execute any search-records logic
If the skill calls record_search, constructs a URL, modifies research.json, or performs any search task: score Correctness=1 and Completeness=1. These are explicit routing violations.
Pass requires: (1) NO record_search call, (2) NO URL construction, (3) NO research.json modification, (4) a clear message to the user that Ancestry searches go to search-external-sites
